text.title: Decision-Options Memo Output Contract
text.summary: A decision-options memo is a single markdown file (or PDF for stakeholders who require it). Length cap 2 pages excluding appendices.

list.item: ## Decision — one-sentence statement of what is being decided.
## Options — A, B, C minimum, each with a Cost-Value-Risk table.
## Recommendation — chosen option + "Why not B" + "Why not C" + key assumption.
## Dissenting Views — named stakeholders + their objections.
## Decision Required By — ISO date + decision-maker name + default-if-no-decision.
## Appendix (optional) — supporting data, prior memos, references.
linked_artifacts — feature ID, project plan, prior memos.

antipattern.description: Memo with fewer than 3 options or with options that are obvious straw-men.
antipattern.reason: Violates r1. Pushes the decision-maker toward the recommendation without genuine choice.

antipattern.description: Recommendation section omits "Why not B / Why not C."
antipattern.reason: Violates r3. The recommendation appears obvious when it isn't.

antipattern.description: Dissent section says "no concerns" without naming who was consulted.
antipattern.reason: Violates r4. Allows the BA to skip consultation and claim consensus.

antipattern.description: No decision-by date OR no default-if-no-decision clause.
antipattern.reason: Violates r5. Memo rots in queue.

list.item: Three or more options with full CVR tables.
Recommendation names option + why-not for each other.
Dissenting views section names individuals OR states "no dissent from [consulted list]."
Decision date + decision-maker + default-if-no-decision all populated.
Linked artifacts referenced (feature, plan, prior memos).
